label: 3T Atlas
title:
3I Atlas was one of the most controversial and viral topics in science in 2025. On July 1st of that year, a telescope of the planet defense complex in Chile observed a point moving in a strange trajectory. It was called 3I Atlas, a comet coming from outer space, the third interstellar object ever discovered to be specific. Its popularity increased exponentially after the publishing of a scientific article called “Is the interstellar object 3I/ATLAS alien technology?” whose main author was Abraham Loeb (Avi Loeb), a Harvard professor who had worked on similar hypotheses before. The paper noticed that comet’s properties such as the trajectory, the angle of entering and the approaching to specific planets were particularly unlikely. Furthermore, the relative position between 3I Atlas and the Earth in its closer point to the sun, for the researchers could be perfect as an strategical pivot for starting an invasion.

A few days after the publication of the article, several news articles began to flood the web. Shocking headlines like “The latest image from 3I Atlas sparks all sorts of theories while NASA remains silent”, “NASA activated the planetary defense protocol against comet 3I/ATLAS” or “Comet 3I/ATLAS revives Stephen Hawking's incredible warning about aliens” went viral in question of hours throughout all cyberspace.

In addition, although the new evidence and the data that confirmed 3I Atlas was just a strange comet, famous personalities like Elon Musk and Kim Kardashian posted in their X and Instagram profiles their own hypotheses, while Avi Loeb kept making sensationalist statements, causing even more polemics around the interstellar visitor and wave after wave of disinformation.

label: Astrology
title:
One of the most popular and ingrained traditions in Western culture is the Horoscope. A system of beliefs built upon the following thesis: The positions of the planets have the power to influence human life and we can predict what will happen to people based on it. The idea is so present in our culture that almost every radio program, newspaper, morning show, web news portal, app store or library has a section dedicated to astrology. According to a Pew Research Center’s survey, approximately 27% of the adult population in the USA do believe in Astrology and consult the horoscope frequently.

Classic western astrology was born thousands of years ago in Egyptian or Babilonyan civilizations and was adapted by the Greeks as a way to understand the universe. Greeks adapted this framework of beliefs and developed the theory of modern astrology almost 2000 years ago, including zodiacal signs, their associated elements and the dates for each one.

However, astrology is not more than a pseudoscientific theory. There is no evidence that proves the predictions are accurate at all. On the contrary, studies have proven astrology does not work better than randomness. Also, the supposed accurate results coming from horoscopes correspond to psychological phenomena such as the Barnum or Forer effect which suggest that vague descriptions are capable of fitting for almost any person.

Moreover, due to the dynamic movements of the Earth and solar system around the galaxy, stars never stay quiet in the same place, so that planets never pass through the same constellation after a couple thousand years. Then, the zodiacal signs we have are wrong, because our sky is not the same sky Greeks observed millennia ago.

label: Chemtrail
title:
According to the conspiracy theory, governments, militaries, or powerful secret organizations are deliberately spraying toxic chemicals or biological agents into the atmosphere from commercial and military airplanes.
Proponents to the conspiracy theory argue that these alleged sprayings are done for sinister purposes, such as:
Weather modification or climate control.
Mind control or psychological manipulation.
Population control or mass sterilization.

Believers of the theory argue that “normal” plane trails should disappear within seconds, and they claim that any trail stays in the sky for a long time, spreads out, or forms grid-like patterns must be a “chemtrail”.
In reality, the lines in the sky are contrails, which is short for condensation trails [1]. They are a completely natural physical phenomenon [2]. In this section, we will delve into how this works: how they form and why some remain while others disappear, drawing on scientific principles.

label: COVID
title: Some fictitious and pseudoscientific claims, as well as conspiracy theories, were associated with the spread of COVID-19 at the beginning of the pandemic, because the disease was new and information was changing rapidly. Those who believed these theories spread misinformation, claiming that the virus was the result of laboratory manipulation, that it had been created to generate profit through the distribution of new vaccines, or that it contained a microchip to manipulate society. This had devastating consequences for how people cared for themselves and their loved ones, due to a lack of trust in the medical profession.

label: Consciousness of a fly
title: In recent months, headlines and social media posts claimed that scientists had 'uploaded a fly's consciousness into a computer' or even created a 'digital living organism.' These statements quickly spread online, but they do not accurately describe what was achieved. In reality, Eon Systems developed a virtual fruit fly controlled by a brain model derived from the complete connectome of a real fruit fly. The connectome, which contains approximately 140,000 neurons and 50 million synaptic connections, was integrated with a simulated body capable of interacting with a virtual environment. As a result, the digital fly was able to perform behaviors such as walking, grooming, and searching for food. The project represents an important step in computational neuroscience because it explores how complex behavior can emerge from the structure of a biological neural network. However, the simulation is not a living organism and does not demonstrate consciousness; it is a computational model designed to study the relationship between brain structure and behavior.

label: Moon Landing
title: One of the most well-known conspiracy theories of the 20th century claims that the human landing on the Moon was a hoax staged by the United States government to feign a technological victory during the Cold War. According to this theory, the Apollo program missions, especially the Apollo 11 moon landing in 1969, were filmed in a television studio and presented to the world as if they had actually taken place on the lunar surface. However, this claim lacks solid evidence and contradicts a wealth of scientific and historical evidence. The moon landing was tracked in real time by observatories and tracking stations in various countries, including some that were political rivals of the United States, such as the then Soviet Union. Furthermore, the Apollo missions brought back hundreds of kilograms of lunar rocks that have been studied by scientists worldwide, and whose characteristics are consistent with an extraterrestrial origin. Photographs, communications records, laser measurements taken to date, and subsequent evidence obtained by space probes that have photographed the landing sites constitute independent proof that astronauts did indeed reach the Moon. For these reasons, the scientific and historical community considers the fake moon landing hypothesis to be an unfounded conspiracy theory, while the arrival of humans on the Moon in 1969 is among the best-documented events in modern history.

label: Numerology
title: Numbers are, above all, an extraordinarily useful tool: symbols that allow us to describe quantities, understand the world, and develop much of human knowledge. However, for centuries, many cultures attributed mystical properties and hidden meanings to them, a speculation that even attracted brilliant figures such as Isaac Newton. Today, the idea that numbers possess magical powers or can influence our destiny has no scientific support, yet it remains a widespread belief. So, what can the experiences of those who have trusted in these ideas tell us, and what can we learn from their stories?

label: Quantum Healing
title: Quantum mechanics is one of the strangest topics in modern physics. Hundreds of students, researchers and professors have tried to understand the laws of quantum physics but, not even the greatest minds of the last century were able to completely explain how that science works because of the complexity and the lack of common sense of its rules. However, not long ago began to gain popularity some new spiritual and healing practices or techniques, based according to their practitioners on quantum mechanics’ results. It was called “Quantum healing”, nowadays it has become part of one of the most popular alternative medicine treatments in the world.

label: Reptilian
title:
The reptilian conspiracy is one of the most famous conspiracy theories in the world; it began in 1999 with the book “ The Biggest Secret: The Book That Will Change the World ” by David Icke ( See Figure 1 ), where David laid out all the bases of Reptilian Theory. The book basically explains two things, the “true world order”, that is ruled by a kind of reptilian men and the real origin of humanity. Both theories are really appealing and they are based on scientific hypotheses and real facts. It became so famous that according to a Reuters/Ipsos poll, one in five people worldwide believed that reptilians were living among humans in disguise in the year 2010.

After a decade, science has evolved and the arguments of the conspiracy have collapsed. Here are some examples of this:

“The missing link in human evolution ”: At the beginning of the 21st century, one of the most topics discussed is the missing link in the evolution, some experts ( such as Ernst Haeckel, who earned a Doctorate in Medicine (M.D.) from the University of Berlin ) stated that the evolutionary line of the humanity was incomplete. These assertions were the origin of “ the theory of the real origin of humans “ , which postulated that the reptilian aliens arrived on Earth and then they genetically modified some Homo habilis ( ancestors of humans ) with reptilian DNA for the creation of intelligent slaves.

These beliefs became even stronger with the research of the American neuroscientist Paul D. MacLean, who theorized that humans possess a reptilian brain through his Triune Brain Theory. Nevertheless, nowadays the mystery around the missing link has been clarified with a better understanding of evolution and a more complete human evolutionary line.

label: Telepathy
title: Due to its implications for physics, biology, and the understanding of the human mind, psychokinesis has been the subject of scientific study for over half a century. Various researchers have attempted to demonstrate its existence through controlled experiments, particularly using random number generators and other systems designed to detect potential mental influences on physical processes. However, the results obtained have been inconsistent and have not been reliably reproducible, a fundamental requirement for validating any scientific phenomenon. For this reason, most scientists believe that the available evidence does not support the existence of psychokinesis and that the apparent demonstrations of this phenomenon can be best explained by experimental errors, cognitive biases, or misinterpretations of statistical coincidences.

label: Terraplanism
title:
Flat Earth theory is often described as one of the oldest fake news in human history. However, the idea that people believed the Earth was flat until 1522 is incorrect. In fact, the spherical shape of the Earth was already proposed in ancient Greece and later accepted among scholars. Today, with modern science and technology, it is well established that the Earth is round. Despite this, some people still support flat Earth ideas and question the scientific evidence with argues such as:

immobility and inertia: If the world turned as fast as science said, humans would fly off the Earth.

La gravedad como pseudofuerza: The gravity does not have any senses, the objects fall to the floor due to the different of density

Bedford Level Experiments: If the Earth had the curvature scientists claim, I could not look at ships far from the coast

To understand this phenomenon, it can be useful to explore these arguments and compare them with established findings in physics and astronomy in a debate.

label: Vaccines
title: The belief that vaccines cause autism is one of the most widespread myths in public health. This idea originated from a study published in 1998 that was later proven fraudulent and retracted from the scientific literature. Since then, numerous studies involving millions of children have consistently found no causal link between vaccines and autism spectrum disorder (ASD). While the exact causes of autism are still being researched, scientific evidence indicates that they are primarily related to genetic and early developmental factors, not vaccination.
